subtopics.title: Table Setup
subtopics.content:
1. **Shuffle all 33 cards.**
2. **Remove 9 cards** face down without looking. Return to box.
3. **Place remaining 24 cards** as face-down deck.
4. **Distribute chips** — 11 chips per player (hide behind hands).

subtopics.title: Player Setup
subtopics.content:
- **11 chips each** — Keep hidden from other players.
- **No starting cards** — Cards are collected during play.

subtopics.title: Game Overview
subtopics.content: No Thanks! is a simple card game where you want the LOWEST score. Flip cards and decide: take the card (and its penalty points) or pay a chip to pass. Consecutive cards in a run only count the lowest value.

subtopics.title: Turn Structure
subtopics.content:
1. **Flip top card** of the deck face up.
2. **Active player decides**: Take the card or pay 1 chip to pass.
3. **Pass** — Place 1 chip on the card. Next player decides.
4. **Take** — Take the card AND all chips on it. A new card is flipped.
5. **No chips** — If you have no chips, you must take the card.

subtopics.title: Key Actions
subtopics.content:
#### Taking a Card
- The card goes in front of you (face up, visible to all).
- Collect all chips that have accumulated on the card.
- Cards represent penalty points equal to their face value.

#### Paying to Pass
- Place 1 chip from your supply onto the card.
- Play passes to the next player.

#### Runs
- Consecutive numbered cards in your collection form a run.
- A run only counts as the LOWEST card in the sequence.
- Example: Having 15, 16, 17 only costs you 15 points (not 48).

subtopics.title: Special Rules
subtopics.content:
- **9 removed cards** — You never know which cards are missing, adding uncertainty.
- **Forced take** — With 0 chips, you must take the current card.
- **Chips are hidden** — Other players don't know exactly how many chips you have.

subtopics.title: Scoring and Endgame
subtopics.content:
#### Game End
- All 24 cards have been taken.

#### Scoring
- Add the face value of all your cards (counting runs as only the lowest card).
- Subtract 1 point for each chip you have.
- **Lowest score wins.**

subtopics.title: Beginner Tips
subtopics.content:
- **Build runs** — Taking a card adjacent to one you already have is cheap.
- **Collect chips** — Taking a card with many chips on it can be profitable.
- **Conserve chips** — Don't waste chips passing on low cards.
- **Watch for gaps** — The 9 removed cards create gaps in potential runs.

subtopics.title: Key Strategies
subtopics.content:
- **Run hunting** — Actively pursue runs; 30+31+32+33 = only 30 points instead of 126.
- **Chip counting** — Track opponents' approximate chip counts. Players with few chips are forced to take.
- **Value threshold** — Cards under 10 are relatively cheap; don't waste many chips avoiding them.
- **Timing takes** — Take cards with lots of chips to replenish your supply.
- **Bluffing** — Pass on cards you'd actually want to collect more chips first.
